etymology.description: 糸(糸) (sī, "silk; thread") + 𧶠 (lù, "to sell; trade") = thread-related concept with phonetic 𧶠 -> continued threadwork
etymology.interpretation: The character combines the semantic element of 糸(糸) (sī, "silk; thread") with the phonetic component 𧶠 (lù, "to sell; trade"), suggesting the idea of maintaining or extending a thread, symbolically extending or continuing something.
etymology.simplification: 續(續)→续(續): 糸(糸) (sī, "silk; thread") was simplified to 纟(纟) as a stroke reduction, and 𧶠 was replaced by 卖(卖) with fewer strokes while keeping phonetic resemblance.

examples.chinese: 我们站在这里聊天(我們站在這裡聊天)，然后继续前进(然後繼續前進)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen zhàn zài zhèlǐ liáotiān, ránhòu jìxù qiánjìn.
examples.english: We stand here chatting, then continue moving forward.

examples.chinese: 他决定继续他的研究(他決定繼續他的研究)。
examples.pinyin: Tā juédìng jìxù tā de yánjiū.
examples.english: He decided to continue his research.

examples.chinese: 即使遇到困难(即使遇到困難)，我们也要继续努力(我們也要繼續努力)。
examples.pinyin: Jíshǐ yù dào kùnnán, wǒmen yě yào jìxù nǔlì.
examples.english: Even when facing difficulties, we must continue to work hard.
